title: Capacitance
short: How much charge a capacitor stores per volt, C = Q / U, measured in farads (F).
full:
Capacitance measures a capacitor's ability to store charge: the charge held per unit voltage, C = Q/U. A larger capacitance stores more charge at the same voltage. It depends on the geometry of the conductors and the insulating material between them.

The energy stored in a charged capacitor is W = ½·C·U².

The SI unit is the farad (F); 1 F = 1 C/V. A farad is large, so real capacitors are often microfarads (µF) or smaller.
